teaching.concept: A main heading names the whole document.
teaching.howTo: Start a line with one hash, add a space, then type the title.

candidates.expectedSkill: Create one top-level hash heading while preserving the supplied title.
candidates.likelyMalformedTrap: Missing the required space after the hash or using a different heading form.
candidates.editorialNote: Keep the title concise, natural in US English, and suitable as a human-readable document title.

teaching.concept: Bold emphasis makes a short phrase stand out inside a document.
teaching.howTo: Wrap only the important words in two asterisks on each side.

candidates.expectedSkill: Apply bold emphasis without changing the protected words.
candidates.likelyMalformedTrap: Using one asterisk, leaving the closing pair out, or bolding surrounding punctuation.
candidates.editorialNote: Reserve bold for genuinely important phrases; this family stays blocked until its real predicate and fixtures exist.

teaching.concept: A list turns parallel items into a scannable group.
teaching.howTo: Start each item on its own line with a hyphen and a space.

candidates.expectedSkill: Create a flat unordered list while preserving every item.
candidates.likelyMalformedTrap: Omitting the space after a marker or joining multiple items on one line.
candidates.editorialNote: Items should be parallel, concise, and easy for a human reader to scan; grading support is not implemented yet.

teaching.concept: A blockquote separates quoted or called-out language from the surrounding text.
teaching.howTo: Start the quoted line with a greater-than sign and a space.

candidates.expectedSkill: Create one blockquote while preserving the supplied sentence.
candidates.likelyMalformedTrap: Omitting the space after the marker or using the marker as ordinary text.
candidates.editorialNote: The sentence should read naturally as a quotation or callout; deterministic support is still pending.

teaching.concept: Inline code distinguishes a command, file, key, or identifier from the sentence around it.
teaching.howTo: Wrap the exact technical token in one backtick on each side.

candidates.expectedSkill: Mark only the protected technical token as inline code while preserving its sentence.
candidates.likelyMalformedTrap: Missing the closing backtick or splitting a multiword command across separate code spans.
candidates.editorialNote: The surrounding sentence must remain natural and the exact technical token must stay intact; engine support is pending.

candidates.likelyMalformedTrap: Missing the closing backtick or splitting a multiword command across separate code spans.
candidates.editorialNote: The surrounding sentence must remain natural and the exact technical token must stay intact; engine support is pending.

teaching.concept: Inline code distinguishes a command, file, key, or identifier from the sentence around it.

teaching.concept: A horizontal rule marks a clear change between two parts of a document.
teaching.howTo: Leave a blank line, type three hyphens on their own line, then leave another blank line.

candidates.expectedSkill: Separate two protected prose blocks with one horizontal rule.
candidates.likelyMalformedTrap: Placing hyphens directly below prose, where Markdown can interpret them as a Setext heading.
candidates.editorialNote: Never teach a bare rule without context; both human-readable prose blocks must be preserved. Engine support is pending.

teaching.concept: A link gives a destination a descriptive name that makes sense in a sentence.
teaching.howTo: Put the label in square brackets and the destination immediately after it in parentheses.

candidates.expectedSkill: Create a descriptive Markdown link that preserves both label and trusted destination.
candidates.likelyMalformedTrap: Showing a raw URL, using an empty label, or separating the brackets from the parentheses.
candidates.editorialNote: Destinations are official public documentation, not invented example URLs; verify currency again before publication. Engine support is pending.

teaching.concept: An image needs descriptive alternative text so its meaning survives when the image cannot be seen.
teaching.howTo: Start with an exclamation mark, put accurate alt text in brackets, and the image path in parentheses.

candidates.expectedSkill: Create an image reference with accurate descriptive alt text and the supplied local path.
candidates.likelyMalformedTrap: Leaving alt text empty, writing a filename instead of a description, or omitting the leading exclamation mark.
candidates.editorialNote: Paths are reserved practice assets; every image remains blocked until a licensed asset exists and a human verifies that the alt text matches it.
